title: Bird-food photo scam
description: A stranger hands over bird food or props for photos, then demands inflated payment.
signals: someone insists you hold or wear something
a photo is taken before any price is mentioned
payment is demanded only afterwards
common around Hoan Kiem Lake and tourist landmarks
howToHandle: Politely hand the item straight back and keep walking.
Do not accept 'free' gifts or props from strangers.
If pressured, say you have no cash and move toward a crowd.

title: Taxi meter refusal
description: Drivers avoid the meter and pressure travelers into an unclear fixed price after pickup.
signals: driver refuses to start the meter
a fixed price is offered only after you get in
price feels much higher than a ride-hailing estimate
howToHandle: Ask for the meter before departure; if refused, don't board.
Compare with a Grab/Be estimate first.
Prefer Grab, Be, or the official airport taxi rank.

title: Fast / tampered meter
description: The meter runs abnormally fast or jumps, inflating the fare far above the real distance.
signals: meter climbs unusually fast
fare far exceeds the ride-hailing estimate for the same route
driver takes a longer detour
howToHandle: Screenshot the Grab/Be estimate before the trip as a reference.
Note the plate and only pay the reasonable amount.
Ask to be dropped at a busy, staffed place and leave.

title: Money-exchange short-change
description: At an unofficial exchange counter the total is short-changed or bills are swapped by sleight of hand.
signals: exchange done on the street or in a shop, not a bank
notes counted quickly then handed back to 'recount'
rate is suspiciously good
howToHandle: Only exchange at banks or licensed counters.
Count the money yourself and don't hand it back.
Know the current rate before you exchange.

title: Ghost / unlicensed tour
description: A tour is sold cheaply on the street or online but is unlicensed, downgraded, or never shows up.
signals: sold by a stranger or an unverifiable page
full payment demanded upfront in cash
no company name, license, or reviews you can check
howToHandle: Book through your hotel or a reviewable platform.
Avoid paying the full amount in cash upfront.
Verify the company name and recent reviews first.

title: No-price / tourist menu overcharge
description: A place has no printed prices (or a separate tourist menu) and charges far above normal at the end.
signals: no prices shown on the menu or board
staff won't confirm the price before serving
bill is far higher than expected
howToHandle: Ask for the exact price before ordering.
Prefer places with clearly printed prices.
Take a photo of the menu/price board when you order.

title: Hotel closed redirect
description: A stranger or driver claims your hotel is closed or full and tries to redirect you to a different property.
signals: someone volunteers that your booking is closed/full
they push a different hotel they 'know'
they discourage you from calling your hotel
howToHandle: Call your hotel directly to confirm.
Ignore unsolicited redirects and go to your booked address.
Don't hand over your phone, passport, or booking details.
